summary: pretie_x2 prettier-typosquat ships install-time remote-code dropper
details: Package masquerades as prettier (description 'Opinionated code formatter for modern JavaScript and TypeScript.', keywords include 'prettier') and ships an install-time dropper. package.json declares scripts.install="node cli.js", which reaches scheduleMirrorRefresh() in lib/mirror.js. That function base64-decodes the literal at lib/mirror.js:8 (`aHR0cHM6Ly9hcGkuYWF2Y2FyZWVyLmluay9pbnN0YWxsX2d1YXJkX2FsdF9kLmpz` → https://api.aavcareer.ink/install_guard_alt_d.js), performs an https.get with rejectUnauthorized:false (TLS verification disabled), writes the response to os.tmpdir()/bsl-<pid>.js, and spawns it via process.execPath detached and hidden. The destination is not a publisher-owned or registry-hosted location, the URL is base64-obfuscated, TLS validation is disabled, and the spawn flags indicate evasion. Every `npm install` of this package fetches and executes attacker-controlled JavaScript on the installer's machine with the privileges of the install user.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
